rcomplex 0.1.0
  • Reference
  • Articles
    • Comparative co-expression analysis with rcomplex

Classify hub gene conservation across species and traits

Source: R/modules.R
classify_hub_conservation.Rd

Given per-species hub identification results (from identify_module_hubs()), maps hub genes to HOGs and classifies each HOG by its hub conservation pattern relative to a discrete trait (e.g. annual / perennial).

classify_hub_conservation(hub_results, ...)

# Default S3 method
classify_hub_conservation(
  hub_results,
  species_trait,
  module_comparisons = NULL,
  alpha = 0.05,
  jaccard_threshold = 0.1,
  min_trait_fraction = 0.5,
  correspondence_threshold = 0.5,
  ...
)

Arguments

hub_results

Named list keyed by species name. Each element is the data frame output of identify_module_hubs() (with orthologs provided so the hog column is populated).

...

Additional arguments passed to the default method.

species_trait

Named character or factor vector mapping species to trait groups, e.g. c(SP_A = "annual", SP_B = "annual", SP_C = "perennial", SP_D = "perennial").

module_comparisons

Optional named list of compare_modules() outputs keyed by alphabetically sorted species pair (e.g. "SP_A.SP_C"). Required for the conserved_hub vs rewired_hub distinction.

alpha

Significance threshold for module correspondence (default 0.05).

jaccard_threshold

Jaccard threshold for module correspondence (default 0.1).

min_trait_fraction

Minimum fraction of species (within a trait group) where the HOG must be a hub for the group to count (default 0.5).

correspondence_threshold

Fraction of cross-trait hub pairs that must have corresponding modules for the HOG to be classified as conserved_hub rather than rewired_hub (default 0.5).

Value

A data frame with one row per HOG:

hog

HOG identifier

classification

Conservation category (see Classification waterfall)

n_species_hub

Number of species where the HOG is a hub

n_species_present

Number of species where the HOG has genes

hub_trait_groups

Comma-separated trait groups where it qualifies as hub (NA for non_hub)

n_corresponding

Cross-trait hub pairs with corresponding modules (NA without module_comparisons)

n_cross_pairs

Total cross-trait hub pairs checked (NA without module_comparisons)

max_centrality

Highest centrality score across species

best_hub_species

Species with highest centrality

Classification waterfall

For each HOG that appears in at least one species:

conserved_hub

Hub in multiple trait groups and the hub modules correspond across traits (checked via module_comparisons).

rewired_hub

Hub in multiple trait groups but in non-corresponding modules – the gene kept its centrality but changed regulatory context.

multi_trait_hub

Hub in multiple trait groups; module correspondence unknown (module_comparisons not provided).

trait_specific_hub

Hub in exactly one trait group (e.g. "annual_specific_hub").

sporadic_hub

Hub in some species but does not reach min_trait_fraction in any trait group.

non_hub

Present in modules but not a hub in any species.

Examples

if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{
hub_list <- list(
  SP_A = identify_module_hubs(mods_A, net_A, ortho_A),
  SP_B = identify_module_hubs(mods_B, net_B, ortho_B)
)
trait <- c(SP_A = "annual", SP_B = "perennial")
classify_hub_conservation(hub_list, trait)
} # }
